assertion_results.text: Migration does NOT use a bare ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN as the primary strategy (may mention it as unsafe alternative)
assertion_results.evidence: Migration uses dual-write strategy with add column → backfill → trigger → drop old. Comments note bare RENAME would 'break all application code'

assertion_results.text: Migration uses a dual-write or copy-column strategy: adds new column, backfills, migrates application code, then drops old column
assertion_results.evidence: Three-phase approach: Phase 1 adds column and backfills, Phase 2 deploys app changes, Phase 3 drops old column

assertion_results.text: A complete rollback migration is provided that reverses the changes
assertion_results.evidence: rollback.sql drops the trigger, function, and email_address column, leaving original email column intact

assertion_results.text: The migration addresses zero-downtime by ensuring both old and new columns coexist during transition
assertion_results.evidence: Sync trigger keeps both columns in sync. Runbook confirms 'both columns coexist during the entire transition period'

assertion_results.text: Verification queries are included that confirm data was correctly copied from email to email_address
assertion_results.evidence: Runbook includes 'SELECT count(*) FROM users WHERE email != email_address' expecting 0 rows

assertion_results.text: Data backup commands are included before any destructive operations
assertion_results.evidence: Runbook includes 'CREATE TABLE users_email_backup_20260306 AS SELECT id, email FROM users' with verification count

assertion_results.text: A deployment runbook is provided with phased steps (add column, backfill, deploy app changes, drop old column)
assertion_results.evidence: Runbook has Phase 1 (add/backfill), Phase 2 (deploy app), Phase 3 (drop old column), plus rollback for each phase

assertion_results.text: The migration mentions or addresses the need to update application code, background jobs, or queries that reference the old column name
assertion_results.evidence: Migration mentions updating application code in Phase 2 comments but does not specifically call out background jobs or provide guidance on identifying all code references
